title: sorghum halepense var. crupina
intro: Sorghum halepense var. crupina, also known as French broom or Spanish broom, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and slender branches. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in the Mediterranean region, and is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Sorghum halepense var. crupina include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular remedy for colds, flu, and other infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to digestive issues.
